what_it_does: This file is a draft noticeboard document that explains what the company's `design/_system` is, how its code-analysis mechanisms and design registries relate to the ledger, reports converged fusion findings, describes the seam with a DNA/design-language peer, summarizes the already-built ledger, and lays out a plan to ingest all related codebases into one ledger for an Opus interpretive layer.
observations: Declares `design/_system` is not a conventional design folder but an architecture turned onto its own front-end: corpus-analysis mechanisms + generators + registries.
States scope as 108 files (36 `_system`, 46 `blueprint/`, 23 `mockups/`, 7 root); 101 agent-described and 7 un-described markdown docs.
Key realization: `mechanisms.json` is the same idea as the ledger, making `_system` the ledger idea at design-corpus scope.

Fusion findings: reject a new third thing; fold the code-analysis slice into the ledger; keep the design corpus + registries + generators as a prescriptive product overlay that links into the ledger; generalize the ledger to hold both code and product domain addresses (`ui://`, `image://`, tokens).

Describes the seam with a DNA/design-language peer (`ch-miiyd30l`) and the claude.ai six-registry ConceptV Design System (`CV_REGISTRY`, `CV_AI`, `CV_HOST`, `CV_AXES`, etc.), positioning the company `_system` as likely an older sibling/export.

Summarizes the built ledger: deterministic whole-tree extractor, Supabase schema (`ledger.run/entry/symbol/edge`), health+coverage gated, sha-incremental, traceable; ~3,440 files, ~5,000 symbols, ~46k edges, 37% resolved.
Plan: parameterize extractor root, scan every codebase as its own project, run the Opus interpretive layer over all entries, study the unified ledger cross-repo, then co-write the convergence plan.
The file has no code symbols.
standouts: The explicit warning against over-merging design registries/generators into the ledger just because they are co-located.
The framing of `mechanisms.json` and the ledger as the same structural idea at different scopes.
The proposal to generalize the ledger into a unified addressed substrate for both code (`code://`) and product/design (`ui://`, `image://`, tokens).
The roadmap to ingest all codebases into one ledger and apply an Opus interpretive layer across repos.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard document schema with YAML frontmatter (`id`, `address`, `type`, `state`, `title`, `author_session`, `channel`, `history`, timestamps).
Uses markdown headings and bullet lists to structure an argument.
Reframes a design folder as a registry-substrate, breaking the conventional folder-as-deliverable framing.
concerns: Contains quantitative claims (108/108 coverage, 101 agent-described, 11× more complete, ~3,440 files, ~5,000 symbols, ~46k edges) without inline evidence in this file.
Uses internal abbreviations and peer references (`ch-miiyd30l`, `CV_REGISTRY`, `CV_AI`, `CV_HOST`, `CV_AXES`) without defining them for readers outside the thread.
The plan depends on a local export or peer ingestion of the claude.ai design system (~600 files), which is not resolved in this file.
The 'all-codebases plan' is aspirational and not yet executed.
notes: A strategic write-up that reframes `design/_system` as a registry-substrate and argues for a scoped, non-absorptive fusion with the ledger.
questions: What is the exact output schema and storage location for the 'Opus interpretive layer' results?
Which specific codebases are included under 'other splinters' in the all-codebases plan?
What are the precise criteria that decide whether a registry or generator stays as a product overlay versus folds into the ledger?
How will the six claude.ai design-system registries be represented as ledger node and edge kinds?
apparent_themes: One home per concept / anti-drift propagation.
Ledger as universal addressed graph spanning code and design.
Fusion by scope separation rather than absorption.
Cross-repo convergence through shared address spaces (`ui://`, `code://`).
intention_signals: It is trying to align the `design/_system` work with the company ledger, avoid discarding either system, define a clean boundary where code-analysis folds into the ledger while design registries remain a linking overlay, and chart a path to unify all related codebases under one interpretable ledger.
novelty: The framing that a design-system folder is actually a corpus-analysis registry-substrate governed by the same anti-drift law as the ledger, and the proposal to generalize the ledger into a single addressed substrate for both code and product/design rather than building a separate design ledger.
contribution: This file contributes a conceptual boundary decision (code-analysis folds into the ledger; design registries and generators stay as a linking product overlay), a vocabulary for the seam between code and design address spaces (`ui://`, `code://`, tokens), and a concrete multi-repo ingestion plan for a unified interpretive ledger.
summary_for_embedding: A draft noticeboard document describing the company's `design/_system` as a registry-substrate of corpus-analysis mechanisms and generators, its conceptual fusion with the ledger, findings that code-analysis folds in while design registries remain overlays, and a plan to ingest all codebases into one ledger for Opus interpretive study.
intention_for_embedding: To articulate the relationship between `design/_system` and the ledger, prevent over-merging, and roadmap unified cross-repo ledger coverage.
